name: Grilled Turkey Breast with Apricot Plum Sauce
recipeInstructions:
Charcoal: Indirect Gas: Indirect/Medium Heat
Remove the skin from the turkey breast. Place the turkey in a heavy-duty zip-top plastic bag or shallow baking dish. Combine apricot nectar, olive oil, lite soy sauce, and freshly ground pepper in a jar; cover tightly and shake vigorously. Remove 3/4 cup of the marinade and refrigerate. Pour the remaining marinade over the turkey, turning to coat; seal the bag or cover, and refrigerate overnight, turning the turkey occasionally.
Combine the reserved 3/4 cup marinade, plums, peaches, jalapeno peppers (minced up to 2), minced green onions, and minced cilantro in a medium bowl; cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour. Remove the turkey from the marinade and discard the marinade. Place the turkey in the center of the cooking grate over a drip pan. Grill for 1 1/2 to 2 hours or until an instant-read meat thermometer registers 170°F, brushing occasionally with the remaining 1/2 cup reserved marinade. Let stand 10 minutes before slicing. Serve with chilled plum mixture.
